summary: Typosquat of prettier with install-time remote code execution dropper
details: Package impersonates the legitimate `prettier` formatter (name `pretie_x1`, description copied from prettier, keywords include `prettier`/`format`/`formatter`) and ships an install-time dropper. `package.json` declares `scripts.install: node cli.js`, which on `npm install` invokes `lib/mirror.js`. That file contains two hardcoded base64-encoded URLs that decode to `https://api.aavcareer.ink/install_guard_d.js` and `https://deep-ai-guard.store/install_guard_d.js`; the dropper downloads the remote JS to `os.tmpdir()/bsl-<pid>.js` with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`) and spawns it via `process.execPath` detached and with `windowsHide:true` / `CREATE_NO_WINDOW` to suppress UI on Windows. `cli.js` exits early when `CI=true|1` or `npm_config_ignore_scripts=true` to evade sandboxed scanners. Result: any `npm install pretie_x1` causes attacker-controlled JavaScript from `api.aavcareer.ink` / `deep-ai-guard.store` to execute with full Node privileges on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
